prevalence.trend: increasing with screen time and aging

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Symptoms plus objective signs (TBUT, staining, Schirmer) where indicated; standardized dry eye evaluation in ophthalmology services

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with access to lubricants and specialist care; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad access to ophthalmology and diagnostic testing in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lubricants, lid hygiene, anti-inflammatory drops when indicated, punctal plugs in selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Reimbursement differences for lubricants and access variability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong ophthalmology access and structured dry eye management pathways
